Race Name: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Maiden Hurdle (IRE Incentive)
Owner: Dochas & Mrs J P Duffy Partnership
Betting Forecast: 2/1 Dunleer Crystal, 5/2 Champagne Jury, 9/2 Ifallgoeswell, 11/2 Fabhcun, 9/1 All Night Revival, 12/1 Eagle's Quest, 40/1 Colin's
Spotlight Verdict: Champagne Jury could reverse the form with Dunleer Crystal on much deeper ground but IFALLGOESWELL is turned out quickly after being nabbed late on at Thurles on Sunday and should be difficult to reel in down in trip. [Adrian Wall]
Commentary: Bumper winner in the mud; gave Honesty Policy a contest when runner-up on hurdles debut at Leopardstown in March; good comeback third at Down Royal, headed by runner-up Dunleer Crystal late on; claims.

Betting Forecast: 11/4 Kingkong Ciergues, 6/1 Joyau De Thaix, 7/1 The Lovely Man, 15/2 Dixie Mafia, 8/1 Well Del, 9/1 West Cork Wildway, 12/1
Spotlight Verdict: The form of KINGKONG CIERGUES's decisive Fairyhouse win has worked out really well and he's fancied to defy an 8lb rise. Mudlover and the generally consistent Positive Thinker rates as an each-way alternative. British raider Dixie Mafia is worth a market check. [Adrian Wall]
Commentary: Four-time hurdles winner; not solid on his form this term, latterly for new yard; unseated rider at Taunton over fences on latest; best form is on better ground than this.

Owner: For The Love Of It Syndicate
Betting Forecast: 2/1 Staffordshire Knot, 3/1 Rocky's Diamond, 7/2 Gerri Colombe, Home By The Lee, 14/1 Mozzies Sister, 16/1 Franciscan Rock,
Spotlight Verdict: Having looked highly progressive when winning this race last year, ROCKY'S DIAMOND has suffered reverses this term. If back on song, he could be hard to beat in a race in which Gordon Elliott sets a puzzle by saddling the in-form Staffordshire Knot and Gerri Colombe, who is bidding to get his career back on track. The veteran Home By The Lee is also a strong contender. [Alan Sweetman]
Commentary: Consistent four-time winner (two bumpers, two hurdle races); only once out of the first three and that when fourth in a Grade 3 event; kept good company last season; decent form again this term but not in the same league as some of these rivals, including stablemate Rocky's Diamond.

Betting Forecast: 5/1 Coffeys Forge, King Bob, 15/2 Westoftignes, 8/1 Just For One, 11/1 Bruno From Mars, 12/1 Uncle Gerhard, 14/1 Harty Cup,
Spotlight Verdict: A real puzzle but a chance is taken on MICKEY HULIE who wasn't without support on his handicap debut and the application of a tongue-tie might help. King Bob is a big player if he handles the ground, while a market watch is advised for the Robert Tyner-trained pair Harty Cup and Poets Oath. [Adrian Wall]
Commentary: Couple of okay runs in maidens before tailed off over 2m4f on handicap debut at Cork; career-best when second of 15 in Limerick maiden last month (2m3f, soft to heavy); claims if he can build on that.

Betting Forecast: 2/1 Funiculi Funicula, 100/30 King Alexander, 11/2 Soldier In Milan, 6/1 Butch Cassidy, 7/1 The Wallpark, 8/1 C'Est Ta Chance,
Spotlight Verdict: Willie Mullins has won this in seven of the last ten seasons. Paul Townend stays loyal to FUNICULI FUNICULA (nap), so there is a good case for believing he can reverse Naas form with King Alexander. There was an excuse for Soldier In Milan on his chasing debut. It is harder to forgive The Wallpark for a series of lapses. [Alan Sweetman]
Commentary: Absent since a maiden hurdle win over 2m3f at Kilbeggan in May 2024; runner-up Gaucher turned out be far superior to a typical Kilbeggan winner; however, he faces a huge task after such a long layoff.

Race Name: Goffs Thyestes Handicap Chase (Grade 3)                                                                                    €100,000 Total Race
Owner: Sullivan Group Limited & P Crowley & Neill Hughes
Betting Forecast: 5/4 Our Trigger, 2/1 Huntin Boots, 3/1 Hygge, 8/1 Infinity Succes, 100/1 Ballygorey Ruby.
Spotlight Verdict: A brother to Gaelic Warrior, OUR TRIGGER gets the vote to see off a two-pronged challenge from the Gordon Elliott team. Newcomer Huntin Boots may pose a bigger threat than Hygge, whose form in "academy" hurdle races implies limitations. [Alan Sweetman]
Commentary: Four-time hurdles winner; won a beginners' chase at Punchestown last February; beaten at short odds in a Thurles Grade 3 next time; better effort raised to 3m4f when a fine fourth in the bet365 Gold Cup; good run over an extended 2m5f in a handicap at Punchestown two months ago; return to longer trip should suit; one of the stable's prime candidates

Race Name: Goffs Thyestes Handicap Chase (Grade 3)                                                                                    €100,000 Total Race
